titleStmt.title: SII 15.268: original edition by P. B. Desai, Dines Chandra Sircar and G. S. Gai
No. 268. (B.K. No. 202 of 1928-29.) AṆṆIGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab set up near a well called Huḍeda-bhāvi.
This is dated Śaka 1567, Vyaya, Mārgaśira śu. 3, Sunday, corresponding to 1646 A.D., November 29, Sunday, f.d.t. °32. The Śaka year was current.
The ruling king is apparently Muhammad Shah, the Ādilshāhi Sultau who is referred to by the Hindu praśasti and stated to be the overlord of the kingdom of Vidyāpura, i.e., Bijapur.

It records the grant of a cowl exempting the taxes for a period of twelve years to the chiefs and representatives of Rustumpēṭhe, newly built in Aṇṇigere-sīme by Rustummajamasāhēba, son of Raṇadullākāna-sāhēba, who was the agent of the Sultan.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:

rustuṁma �ṭhaya kaṭisidāta sīka Alisā
hēbaru mujimidāra taṁ�gauḍa svā
midēsāya maga parappa dēsakulakaruṇi

seṭi giriyappana maga paramēśvara (ṭise ma)
mahanāḍugaḷige sāhēbaru koṭa kaulu
haṁneraḍu varuṣa 12 birāḍavilla suṁka vi
lla pasikiyilla yi kaulige tappidavara bā
imprecatory passage
sāsanava barada Akasālera būsavana ma
ge śivaṁṇa kaṭadāta tiṁmaṁṇa sūtāri
re seṭi Ali Ahaṁnuda yahaladāra rustuṁmajamā mahalakariThis line is engraved in the left margin.
1. These two lines are engraved on the left side of the same slab.
Digital edition of SII 15.268 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
